topic: Someone finds a wallet containing a large sum of cash
raw_output:
[story 1]

The worn leather of the wallet felt heavy in my palm, the weight of the bills pressing down on my fingers. It was tucked beneath a park bench, and the sheer volume of cash inside made my stomach clench. I quickly scanned the ID inside—a name I didn't recognize—and a wave of intense discomfort washed over me. My hands started to tremble, and I fumbled for my phone, suddenly desperate to get rid of this tangible evidence of a situation I hadn't asked for. Every glance I caught in the direction I found it felt like an accusation. I shoved the wallet deep into my backpack, my movements jerky and overly quick, desperate to erase the encounter.

[story 2]

I picked up the item near the entrance to the coffee shop, and the crispness of the hundred-dollar notes was startling. My breath hitched, a shallow, rapid intake of air. I stared at the stack, the numbers swimming before my eyes. A hot flush crept up my neck, making my skin prickle. I couldn't look at the surrounding area; I focused entirely on the money, my jaw tightening until my teeth ached. I felt an overwhelming urge to disappear, to somehow dissolve into the pavement. I quickly tucked it into my coat pocket, pulling the fabric around me tightly, trying to shrink myself down to nothing.

[story 3]

It was an unexpected find, lying innocently on the sidewalk. When I opened it, the sheer quantity of currency stunned me into silence. My heart hammered against my ribs, a frantic rhythm against the quiet afternoon. I could feel the heat rising across my cheeks, a deep, uncomfortable warmth that spread through my chest. I avoided making eye contact with anyone nearby, focusing instead on the texture of the paper, a desperate attempt to ground myself. With clumsy, hurried movements, I slipped the wallet into my bag, the sound of the zipper feeling deafeningly loud in the sudden quiet of my internal panic.
